title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background is nearly a textbook match for this role — hybrid AI search, search ranking, personalization/recommendation systems, and rigorous A/B experimentation are all core to her Samsung and Kolon experience, and she has direct evidence of shipping at scale (10M+ monthly users, $2B GMV platform). The domain alignment is excellent: Airbnb's R&P team sits exactly at the intersection of search relevance, recommendations, and conversational AI where Riley has spent the last several years. The one honest gap is the 10+ years requirement — Riley has ~9 years total, with her deepest ML/search work spanning roughly 3–4 years, so she's at the low edge of the experience bar but not disqualifying given the quality of her output.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain and archetype fit with Samsung/Kolon evidence directly mapping to Airbnb's R&P mandate; minor seniority gap is unlikely to disqualify a candidate with this quality of output.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems at global scale — shipping hybrid AI search across 3 international marketplaces at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) and ML-driven recommendation engines on a $2B GMV platform. Expert at partnering with ML engineers and applied researchers to define model strategy, experimentation frameworks, and relevance metrics that drive measurable booking and engagement outcomes.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers
gap_handling: The 10+ year requirement is the only real flag — Riley has ~9 years. Frame tenure confidently: her LinkedIn headline and resume summary should lead with "9+ years" and emphasize that her 3+ years of dedicated ML/search/personalization work (Samsung + Amazon) directly satisfies the depth requirement. In a cover letter, acknowledge the scope ("I've owned strategy for a globally distributed AI search platform, not just features") rather than year count.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core competencies — AI-powered discovery, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at scale — map almost perfectly to what Airbnb is building on its homepage. The Samsung experience (10M+ monthly users, hybrid search, conversational AI, product discovery funnels) and the Kolon e-commerce background (recommendation systems, CTR optimization, conversion funnel) provide direct, quantified evidence for this exact domain. The primary gap is consumer marketplace / travel context (Airbnb's unique supply-demand two-sided marketplace) and a modest stretch on the "product narrative / marketing partnership" axis, which Riley has touched but not headlined.
recommendation_reason: 9 years of AI-powered discovery and search at consumer scale is a near-perfect domain match; strong quantified evidence across every core requirement.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search, discovery, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including a hybrid AI search platform serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers, data scientists, and UX teams to build AI-powered browsing experiences that measurably lift engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 → $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The JD emphasizes "product narrative / marketing storytelling" and lifecycle campaign partnership — areas Riley has touched (AEO pipeline, conversational AI GTM, CRM marketing) but not headlined. In the resume and cover letter, explicitly frame the AEO pipeline launch (8x AI agent traffic, +40% conversion in 12 weeks) and the conversational AI chat experience as examples of crafting product narrative and driving cross-functional marketing alignment, not just engineering delivery.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience maps tightly to this role's 6+ year requirement, with deep hands-on credentials in e-commerce discovery, personalization/recommendation systems, and A/B-driven experimentation — all explicitly cited as preferred qualifications. The Shopping Experience / consumer discovery domain aligns strongly with Riley's Samsung.com and Kolon work, and the zero-to-one framing is supported by multiple end-to-end launch examples. The only meaningful gap is the health/nutrition/wellness domain: this is a Health-focused product area, and Riley has no demonstrated background in that vertical — though Instacart lists it as preferred, not required.
recommendation_reason: Strong consumer e-commerce, discovery, and experimentation credentials check nearly every required box; health domain gap is preferred-only and manageable with targeted framing.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9 years of experience building AI-powered discovery, personalization, and shopping experiences at Samsung.com and Amazon that serve millions of users and drive measurable conversion and engagement lift. Proven track record of leading zero-to-one launches end-to-end — from user research and roadmap definition through scaled global rollout — in highly cross-functional, fast-moving environments.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing that increased web navigation CSAT/NPS by 20% QoQ and improved engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148→$185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.
gap_handling: The Health domain (dietary filters, nutrition, wellness) is Riley's clearest gap — but it's listed as a preferred qualification, not required. Bridge this in your cover letter by framing your discovery/personalization work as directly transferable: e.g., "helping customers find products that match their specific needs" mirrors health-goal filtering. If you have any personal interest in health/nutrition or have used health data integrations, call that out. Avoid pretending you have regulated health product experience — instead, lean into your zero-to-one launch credentials and fast learning track record.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's core search expertise — hybrid AI search, ranking, relevance metrics, query understanding, and behavioral signal optimization — maps directly onto Faire's Search Algorithms role, and the Samsung work is highly transferable. The main friction is seniority: this is a Staff PM role, which sits above Riley's target (Senior/PM III), and the 7+ years requirement and "decisions alongside founders/CEO" framing signal a high-autonomy, high-scope expectation. Additionally, the domain is B2B wholesale marketplace (brand-to-retailer), not consumer-facing — Riley's Amazon B2B experience provides partial coverage but most of their portfolio is B2C e-commerce search and discovery.
recommendation_reason: Strong search algorithm alignment and ML depth clear the bar, but Staff-level seniority and B2B wholesale domain are real stretches that require deliberate positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience building search, ranking, and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven catalog tools for 2M+ B2B sellers at Amazon. Deep expertise in search relevance metrics, behavioral signal modeling, and experimentation frameworks that directly translate to Faire's mission of powering algorithmic discovery for independent retailers worldwide.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and "decisions alongside founders/CEO" language signal an expectation of principal-level scope and influence. In your cover letter and resume summary, lean hard into the strategic ownership angle — you defined the entire search product vision and roadmap at Samsung.com for 10M+ users, and drove end-to-end launches with cross-functional alignment up to executive level. Quantify your scope of ownership, not just outcomes. For the B2B gap, explicitly connect Amazon's 2M+ seller ecosystem to Faire's retailer-facing discovery problem — both are B2B platforms where catalog quality and search relevance directly drive GMV.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing expertise, and strong data-driven growth instincts are directly relevant to Anthropic's API Growth mandate. However, this role is explicitly growth-PM focused on a developer/API platform — Riley's experience skews heavily toward consumer e-commerce discovery and search, with limited direct exposure to developer tools, API products, usage-based monetization, or B2B SaaS funnels. The Amazon B2B role is the closest analog but lasted under a year. The core gap is developer-platform growth experience, which is a primary requirement.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and growth fundamentals but lacks the developer platform / API growth PM experience this role explicitly requires — apply with targeted framing of Amazon B2B and technical platform work.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years driving growth across AI-powered search, discovery, and ML platform products at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — with a consistent record of owning full-funnel metrics from acquisition through monetization. Experienced shipping developer-facing platform features (Amazon B2B listing tools for 2M+ sellers) and AI-native products powered by LLMs, semantic search, and recommendation systems that scale to 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Defined on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
gap_handling: Lean hard into the Amazon role as your developer/B2B platform proof point — position the ML-driven listing automation as a self-serve, usage-based platform product analogous to API onboarding and activation. In your cover letter, explicitly call out your understanding of developer acquisition funnels (docs, console, billing, upgrade paths) and map your Samsung AEO pipeline work as evidence of building for technical/API-consuming audiences. Consider adding a sentence in the resume summary that names "developer platform" or "self-serve API products" directly to pass keyword filtering.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML personalization background — spanning hybrid search, LLM pipelines, RAG-adjacent retrieval, and recommendation systems — maps strongly to the technical core of this role. The Samsung and Kolon work demonstrates real consumer-scale AI personalization with measurable outcomes, and the AEO pipeline shows fluency with structured data and retrieval architecture. The stretch is seniority: this is a Staff PM role, and while Riley's target includes Staff as a stretch, Airbnb's CS Platform framing (platform product, multi-surface infrastructure, cross-org alignment at Staff level) skews toward a more seasoned platform-builder — Riley's experience is more product-facing than infrastructure-platform-facing. No customer support / CS domain experience is also a gap the JD implicitly prizes.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI personalization and search signals, but this is a Staff-level platform infrastructure role in the CS domain — one level above Riley's target and outside her core domain; worth applying with tailored positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered personalization and search systems at consumer scale — including hybrid search, LLM-driven pipelines, and recommendation platforms serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven ability to partner with ML engineers on retrieval architecture and experiment design, and to translate complex AI signals into clear product roadmaps that drive measurable business outcomes.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit customer support / CX platform experience — reframe Samsung's conversational AI chat experience and on-site support-adjacent discovery flows as the closest analog. Emphasize cross-surface platform thinking (search + recommendations + conversational AI as a unified intelligence layer) to address the "foundational platform product" requirement. Acknowledge in networking conversations that this is a stretch role, but lead with the breadth of AI personalization signals owned end-to-end.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths in AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and growth-oriented experimentation map meaningfully onto the Help Center's AI-chat and discovery-led redesign objectives. However, the role is fundamentally about customer support surface redesign, help content strategy, deflection rate optimization, and CustOps tooling — none of which appear in Riley's resume — making domain alignment partial rather than strong. The NYC hybrid requirement also creates a practical friction point for a Bellevue-based candidate.
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/discovery/growth overlap, but the role's core domain (support enablement, help center, CustOps tooling) is a notable gap; worth applying only with significant tailoring to bridge the narrative.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving discovery-led, AI-powered user experiences across e-commerce and B2B platforms — including conversational AI, personalized recommendation systems, and hybrid search for 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record of transforming siloed product surfaces into cohesive, measurable growth levers, with deep cross-functional ownership across engineering, design, and stakeholder operations.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit experience in customer support surfaces, help center platforms, deflection metrics, or CustOps tooling. To address this gap, frame the conversational AI chat experience as a self-service deflection mechanism, and position the AEO pipeline and search instrumentation work as analogous to structuring and surfacing help content at scale. Acknowledge the domain shift honestly in a cover letter, leaning into transferable AI + discovery + growth credentials.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, SQL/Tableau tooling, and hands-on experience shipping AI-powered systems (hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines) map well to several of Lyft's stated requirements. However, this is a Staff PM role focused on an internal AI *platform* and agentic/multi-agent orchestration — a pure platform/infrastructure archetype that is a meaningful departure from Riley's consumer-facing search, discovery, and e-commerce background. The agentic systems requirement (context engineering, multi-agent orchestration) is the most significant gap, and the role is explicitly one level above Riley's target seniority (Staff PM vs. Senior PM).
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML foundation and tooling overlap, but the agentic platform archetype, Staff-level seniority, and SF hybrid requirement are all meaningful friction points for Riley.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems — from hybrid search and LLM-driven pipelines to recommendation APIs and conversational AI — at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Adept at translating complex ML capabilities into measurable business outcomes, partnering with scientists and engineers to evaluate technology bets, and driving cross-functional adoption of AI tooling at scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — scoping conversation flows, partnering with engineering and UX, and launching in production to support product discovery and comparison for 10M+ monthly users.

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup using LLM-driven pipelines, boosting AI agent traffic 8x and increasing conversion 40% in 12 weeks.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria — improving precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is agentic AI / multi-agent orchestration experience — Lyft explicitly calls this out as a strong requirement. Riley should proactively address this in the cover letter by pointing to the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as adjacent evidence, and consider noting any personal exploration of agentic frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, etc.) in the skills or projects section. The Staff-level seniority is also a stretch; Riley can mitigate this by emphasizing org-wide influence at Samsung (10M+ users, executive-level roadmap ownership, global expansion) rather than team-level execution.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing rigor, and scale credentials (10M+ users, $2B+ GMV) are strong fits for Anthropic's growth environment, and the AI domain alignment is excellent. However, this role is fundamentally a Growth/Monetization PM — pricing strategy, subscription conversion funnels, packaging, and free-to-paid optimization — which is not where Riley's experience is concentrated. Riley's growth work at Kolon (2016–2021) is the closest match but is dated and consumer e-commerce, not SaaS/subscription monetization. There is no clear evidence of owning pricing, packaging, or paid conversion in a subscription business, which is the core requirement.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and scale credentials, but the role demands deep subscription monetization and pricing ownership that Riley hasn't directly demonstrated.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through data-driven experimentation, conversion optimization, and AI-powered product experiences at scale across Samsung.com, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record of defining KPIs, leading A/B testing programs, and building ML-driven systems that directly improve revenue metrics — including a 40% conversion lift in 12 weeks and a 25% AOV increase on a $2B+ GMV platform.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%, boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit SaaS/subscription monetization experience (pricing tiers, paid upgrade flows, consumption billing). To bridge this gap: (1) reframe the Kolon onboarding/retention bullet to emphasize its "free-to-first-purchase funnel" and monetization angle; (2) highlight the AEO pipeline's direct conversion impact (+40%) as a revenue-driving initiative; (3) in the cover letter, directly address the subscription gap by drawing a parallel between e-commerce conversion funnel ownership and SaaS free-to-paid mechanics, and express genuine passion for Anthropic's unique consumer + developer monetization challenge.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience, SQL skills, LLM familiarity, and strong track record with data-intensive systems are genuinely relevant — and the AI search + pipeline work at Samsung is compelling. However, the role is fundamentally B2B SaaS (observability/analytics platform), and Riley's background skews heavily toward consumer e-commerce and retail search. More critically, the role requires deep domain knowledge of BI tooling, semantic layers, data warehouses, and the modern data stack (dbt, Snowflake, Looker, etc.) — areas not evidenced anywhere in the resume. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial build, governance layer design, and BI-competitive landscape research are also outside Riley's demonstrated scope.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product and SQL credentials are a partial match, but missing B2B SaaS depth and BI/data stack domain knowledge create meaningful gaps for a 0-to-1 product at this level.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving LLM-powered data products, search ranking systems, and structured data pipelines at scale — including conversational AI, AEO metadata pipelines, and multi-modal ML workflows at Samsung and Amazon. Experienced shipping 0-to-1 AI experiences that serve both technical and non-technical users, with a strong foundation in SQL, experimentation, and cross-functional product delivery in data-intensive environments.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories
gap_handling: Directly acknowledge the BI/data stack gap in a cover letter by framing your AEO metadata pipeline and structured catalog work as analogous to semantic layer design — you've built systems that normalize and govern structured data for AI consumption at scale. Mention any personal exposure to tools like dbt, Snowflake, or Looker even if exploratory. The "AI-pilled" signal is important — reference specific AI tools you use daily (Cursor, Claude, etc.) to show cultural fit with what they're looking for.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview.
